summary: Name-squat of Cloudflare Workers `worker-build` tooling with no payload
details: The npm package `worker-build@9.0.0` claims a name matching the Cloudflare Workers / workers-rs build helper of the same name, but ships only a README.md and a package.json — no main entry, no lifecycle scripts, and no executable code. The README itself describes the package as a dependency-confusion / name-squatting demonstration against the legitimate `worker-build` tool. Package metadata is empty (`author: ""`, `repository.url: ""`), consistent with a placeholder squat rather than a maintained library. Because the current version has no payload, installing it produces no direct installer-side harm today; the risk is that the namespace is held and a future version under this name could ship arbitrary code to anyone who resolves `worker-build` from the public npm registry expecting the Cloudflare tool.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
